summary: Crystal‑clear soundboard capture of a laid‑back 1976 show with standout performances of Mission In The Rain, Row Jimmy and St Stephen.
review: The recording is an excellent soundboard source that delivers rich, crisp detail with only occasional hiss or level quirks. Musically the concert is hailed as one of ’76’s finest – a mellow groove anchored by a superb second set, highlighted by Mission In The Rain, Row Jimmy and a silky St Stephen, with the whole band locked in tight, soulful play.

ai_rating.rationale: Over 70% of reviews are 5‑star, praising both performance and audio fidelity; a few lower scores note minor issues but do not outweigh the overwhelming acclaim.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Most reviewers call the mix crisp, rich and sweet with excellent instrument separation. A couple of notes mention a faint hiss/noise floor and occasional level imbalance, but overall the sound is clear and very listenable.

standout_songs: Help on the Way > Slipknot! > Franklin's Tower
show_quality.setlist_flow: The set is described as a relaxed, ‘slow‑silky’ groove typical of 1976, with a particularly killer second set that sustains the mellow vibe while delivering tight jams and seamless transitions.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Praised for sweet, soulful guitar tone and vocal performance; one dissenting reviewer felt he was slightly out of time but the consensus is overwhelmingly positive.
band_member_comments.Phil: Bass described as ‘slippery’ and sounding great throughout the show.
band_member_comments.Bob: Vocals (with Donna) noted as being in key; his guitar work receives occasional praise for fitting the mellow vibe.
band_member_comments.Keys: Keith’s piano fills are highlighted as beautiful, prominent in the mix and a key part of the show’s texture.
